meaning: 引入；带来（收益/资源）；招聘
example: The company is trying to bring in new technology to improve efficiency.

meaning: 向某人详细告知某事；给某人补充情况
example: Can you fill me in on what happened at the meeting yesterday?
The committee is composed of experts from different fields.
The story is full of twists and turns that keep readers engaged.
He tried his best to win her affection with thoughtful gestures.
Make sure you don’t leave out any important details in your report.
They are planning to move in next weekend.
Regular exercise can help boost your willpower.
He was desperate for a chance to prove himself.
The result was anything but satisfying.
He did nothing but complain all day.
If you apply yourself to your studies, you will make great progress.
The small boat was at the mercy of the storm.
She quickly took to her new school.
He can’t even swim, let alone dive.
The disease wiped out a large part of the population.
Make sure your software is up to date.
Not once did he mention his achievements.
Education is an important means of improving one’s life.
As the exam draws near, students become more nervous.
She received a flood of messages after posting the news.
We should allow for possible delays when planning the trip.

meaning: 导致；有助于；为……撰稿（已补充你的备注）
example: She contributes regularly to a local newspaper.
The new policy brought about significant changes.
His behavior gave rise to many questions.
I’ll back you up if anyone questions your decision.
He refused to back down in the argument.
She took pains to explain the problem clearly.
In light of recent events, the meeting was postponed.
He declined to answer the question.
